item.title: Hong Kong Stays Hot for Medical Equipment Maker Debut: ECM Watch
item.description: Shares of Star Sports Medicine Co. in their Hong Kong trading debut on Tuesday, extending a run of strong listings in the city in recent months as investors showed that appetite for Chinese stocks extends beyond companies linked to artificial intellgence.

item.title: Drowning in Debt, Eight Million Firms Miss Payments in Brazil
item.description: Take a glance at the stock market, and all appears well in the Brazilian economy. The Ibovespa has soared more than any other major index in the Americas over the past year — almost 60% in dollar terms at last count.

item.title: BNP Banker Fired Over Bullying Allegations Loses His $2 Million Payout
item.description: A senior BNP Paribas SA banker who was fired for allegedly bullying his subordinates on the trading floor lost a €1.7 million ($2 million) unfair-dismissal award on appeal after judges drew on testimony from staffers.

item.title: Chrysalis Writes Down Stake in UK Digital Bank Starling by 14%
item.description: Chrysalis Investments Ltd. cut the value of its stake in UK financial technology firm Starling Bank, as the conflict in the Middle East impacted stock markets and the digital bank’s peers.

item.title: Israel-Focused Energean Looks to Africa in Diversification Push
item.description: Energean Plc is planning to scale up operations in Africa to diversify from its focus on the Middle East as conflict in the region roils energy markets.

item.title: Nscale Plans Expansion of Microsoft Data Center Site in Portugal
item.description: Nscale Global Holdings has agreed to spend €695 million ($812 million) on infrastructure in Portugal in an expansion of its partnership with Microsoft Corp.

item.title: Bridge Growth Raises $790 Million to Extend Hold on Solace
item.description: Bridge Growth Partners has raised $790 million of equity in a secondary transaction that will let it hold the infrastructure software company Solace for longer.

item.title: Toronto Home Sales Increase Most in Nine Months as Buyers Return
item.description: Home sales in Toronto posted their biggest increase in nine months as lower prices finally began to lure buyers back to the market.

item.title: India Is Said to Plan New Platform for Bond Derivative Trades
item.description: India is planning an electronic platform for trading bond forwards, according to people familiar with the matter, in a bid to improve price discovery in a market that operates largely over the counter.

item.title: US-Iran Ceasefire Holds After Hormuz Clashes and UAE Strikes
item.description: The fragile US-Iran ceasefire held Tuesday morning after a day of clashes involving shipping in the Strait of Hormuz and missile attacks against the United Arab Emirates.

item.title: UniCredit Opens Its €35 Billion Commerzbank Bid to Investors
item.description: UniCredit SpA has submitted its Commerzbank AG takeover proposal to the target’s shareholders, initiating a six-week period that will decide the German lender’s independence.

item.title: Hong Kong Weathers War Shocks With Fastest GDP Growth Since 2021
item.description: Hong Kong’s economy expanded at its fastest pace in almost five years, as old growth drivers like tourism and retail spending cushioned the blow from the global energy crisis caused by the war in Iran.

item.title: Pakistan Trade Deficit Widens as Imports Surge
item.description: Pakistan’s trade deficit widened more than expected last month as imports surged, adding strain to the fragile economy and thin foreign exchange reserves.

item.title: Bank of Mauritius Sees Inflation Breaching Target by Year-End
item.description: Inflation in Mauritius may breach the upper limit of the central bank’s target range this year as prolonged conflict in the Middle East raises the cost of imported goods, Bank of Mauritius Governor Priscilla Muthoora Thakoor said.

item.title: Mozambique Weighs Swapping Dollar Debt for Yuan in China Talks
item.description: Mozambique is considering converting the $1.4 billion it owes China into renminbi loans as part of a debt restructuring with its biggest bilateral creditor, in line with similar moves by other African sovereign borrowers.

item.title: Thailand Eyes $12 Billion in New Debt to Cushion Mideast Crisis
item.description: Thailand’s government plans to raise 400 billion baht ($12 billion) in new debt to support farmers, low-income households, and small businesses affected by the fallout from the Middle East conflict, while accelerating the country’s shift away from fossil fuels.

item.title: Hon Hai Sales Grow 30% With Expansion of AI Server Business
item.description: Key Nvidia Corp. partner Hon Hai Precision Industry Co. reported a 29.7% increase in April revenue, underscoring persistently strong spending on hardware essential for AI.

item.title: Iraq Slashes Oil Prices for Buyers Willing to Transit Hormuz
item.description: Iraq is offering its term buyers huge discounts for crude loaded this month, but tankers will have to transit the Strait of Hormuz to collect the barrels deep inside the Persian Gulf just as hostilities flare in the region.

item.title: EU Has Tools If US Threatens Industries, French Minister Says
item.description: The European Union has tools that it can turn to if Donald Trump makes excessive threats to strategic industries such as steel, French Trade Minister Nicolas Forissier said.

item.title: EQT Raises Bid for UK Testing Firm Intertek to £8.9 Billion
item.description: EQT AB raised its offer for Intertek Group Plc to roughly £8.9 billion ($12.1 billion) as it ramps up its efforts to acquire the British product-testing company.

item.title: Japan Has Two More Key Windows for Yen Intervention by IMF Rules
item.description: Japan can conduct only two more sessions of three-day interventions by November if it wants to maintain its status of having a freely floating exchange rate, based on International Monetary Fund guidelines.

item.title: Audi Flags Trump Tariff Plan as Threat to Carmaker’s Outlook
item.description: Volkswagen AG’s Audi warned that US President Donald Trump’s push for higher tariffs on European cars may weigh on its financial performance this year.

item.title: Leo Pharma Eyes $800 Million Deal Push as IPO Plans Advance
item.description: Leo Pharma is actively seeking more acquisition targets, with as much as 5 billion kroner ($800 million) to spend on dealmaking as it positions itself for an initial public offering, according to its top management.

item.title: New World Weighs Sale of Stake in $2 Billion HK Hotel Portfolio
item.description: New World Development Co., the cash-strapped property developer, is in talks to sell its 50% stake in a portfolio of three Hong Kong hotels valued at a total of $2 billion, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: ASG Hospital Said to Plan Filing for $500 Million IPO This Month
item.description: ASG Hospital Pvt., an Indian eye-care chain backed by General Atlantic, is planning to file for an initial public offering as early as this month that could raise up to $500 million, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Alphabet Kicks Off Six-Part Euro Debt Offering
item.description: Google parent company Alphabet Inc. has kicked off the sale of a six-part debt sale in euros, according to a person familiar with the matter who asked not to be identified.

item.title: Swiss Inflation Jumps to Fastest Since 2024 on Iran War Fallout
item.description: Switzerland’s inflation quickened to a 16-month high in April as energy costs jumped because of war in the Middle East.

item.title: Election Pledges in India Risk Wider Deficits, Economists Warn
item.description: Implementing populist promises that featured prominently in election wins in key Indian states this week risk straining their finances, with economists warning of wider fiscal deficits.

item.title: Malaysian Minister Calls for Update on ‘Corporate Mafia’ Probe
item.description: A Malaysian minister urged the country’s police chief to clarify the status of investigations into allegations a network of businessmen was colluding with anti-graft agency officials to oust executives.

item.title: BlackRock Touts ETFs as Liquidity Antidote to Private Exposure
item.description: Exchange-traded funds can be the source of liquidity that retail investors need after ramping up exposure to private assets, BlackRock Inc. executives wrote in a report.
